alerts.title: Iran drone strike kills 1, injures 63 at Kuwait International Airport
alerts.summary: Iranian Shahed-136 drone confirmed via CCTV striking Terminal 1 of Kuwait International Airport early June 4, with 1 fatality and 63 injured per incidents feed (The Guardian, Scripps News). Multiple Telegram posts (ME Spectator, ~48K-52K views) document strike with video evidence. Event marks direct Iranian attack on critical civilian infrastructure in US-allied nation amid active ceasefire negotiations.

alerts.title: Iran enrichment steady at 60% U-235; 1-2 week breakout window persists
alerts.summary: Iran maintains uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 with estimated 1-2 week nuclear breakout timeline per global_metrics. IAEA reports continued Iranian non-compliance, barred inspections, and proliferation concerns. Five nuclear headlines (NYT, Bloomberg, France 24, The Hill, Times of Israel) corroborate escalating concern over undisclosed enriched uranium stockpiles amid active Persian Gulf hostilities.

alerts.title: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon-Israel ceasefire; strikes resume across northern Israel
alerts.summary: Despite June 3 ceasefire agreement, Hezbollah resumed rocket and drone attacks on Kiryat Shmona, Nahariyya, Shlomi and northern Israeli settlements, and deployed FPV drones targeting Mediterranean positions per Telegram (ME Spectator, ~44K-46K views). Israel continued operations in southern Lebanon hours after ceasefire renewal per incidents feed (France 24, BBC). Agreement breakdown signals escalation cycle continuation.

alerts.title: US Navy maintains triple-carrier posture amid Iran tensions; submarine deployed to Persian Gulf
alerts.summary: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower, USS Abraham Lincoln, and USS Gerald R. Ford remain deployed across Red Sea, Arabian Sea, and Mediterranean respectively per assets feed. USS Florida submarine positioned in Persian Gulf. Three Red Sea destroyers (USS Carney, USS Mason, USS Laboon) and amphibious ships (USS Bataan) provide sustained forward presence. Configuration reflects heightened readiness posture amid Kuwait airport strike and ceasefire violations.
alerts.region: Persian Gulf / Red Sea / Arabian Sea

alerts.title: US political debate intensifies on Iran response; Trump dominance in discourse peaks
alerts.summary: US sentiment remains elevated negative at 43% vs. positive 47% per sentiment feed. 'Trump' dominates keyword analysis (77 mentions), followed by 'senate,' 'fund,' 'republicans,' 'iran,' 'war,' 'bolton,' indicating fractious domestic debate over administration Iran policy and ceasefire negotiations. Shift reflects politicization of conflict response and internal US alignment tensions.

analysis_note: Strategic moment reflects simultaneous escalation on three fronts—Iranian direct strike on Kuwait airport, Hezbollah ceasefire rejection with resumed operations, and persistent nuclear breakout risk—amid fractious US domestic political debate and maintained high naval deterrent posture, with threat level at critical (9/10).
